extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_b is set true by r4 whenever hepatic_impairment is true. rec_drug_a is set true by r5 only when (hepatic_impairment AND low_ejection_fraction AND elderly AND immunosuppressed AND anticoagulated AND hypertension) all hold. Crucially, r5's guard INCLUDES hepatic_impairment, so any input that fires r5 necessarily also fires r4. These rules set different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so there is no priority/tie conflict to suppress either one — both outputs are set to true simultaneously. Setting hepatic_impairment, low_ejection_fraction, elderly, immunosuppressed, anticoagulated, and hypertension all true (the other three inputs only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant, set to false): r4 fires -> rec_drug_b=true; r5 fires -> rec_drug_a=true. Therefore rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b = true, violating mutual exclusion. The defaults do not help because both outputs are explicitly set. The property is violated.
